title: Recast as discrete-time hazard at the stint level instead of plain binary next-lap classification
summary: This target is naturally a discrete event-time problem: each row is the hazard of a stop on the next lap given survival up to now.
rationale: This target is naturally a discrete event-time problem: each row is the hazard of a stop on the next lap given survival up to now. Discrete-time survival work shows standard classifiers can outperform continuous-time alternatives when trained on person-period style hazard targets, and hazard parameterizations often beat PMF-style formulations.
implementation_hint: Create stint-level sequences per `(Year,Race,Driver,Stint)` and train a hazard model with lap-bin features plus cumulative survival context, e.g. `p(stop next lap | not yet stopped this stint)`. Add monotone constraints on tyre-age or degradation-derived features if the learner supports them. Blend the hazard score with the current classifier rather than replacing it immediately.
expected_effect: Should better model sharply rising end-of-stint risk and produce cleaner probabilities near pit windows.
risk: If the current label already behaves almost Markovian with existing features, the reformulation may add complexity without clear gain.
